era_context: The early 16th century is marked by the Renaissance in Europe, the Ottoman Empire's consolidation of power in the Mediterranean and Near East, the Ming Dynasty in its mature phase, and European colonial expansion accelerating in the Americas and Asia. The Reformation is still five years away. Indigenous American civilisations remain independent of European control.

events.title: Selim I becomes Ottoman Sultan; deposition of Bayezid II
events.description: Selim I, known as 'Selim the Grim,' became Ottoman Sultan in April 1512 after forcing the abdication of his father, Bayezid II. The succession was contested and involved military conflict between Selim and his brothers. Selim I's ascension marked a significant turning point in Ottoman history, as he was known for his military ambitions and his aggressive expansion policies. Bayezid II, who had ruled for 31 years, was forced to abdicate due to his age and his perceived weakness in dealing with internal Ottoman factions and external threats. Selim I's reign would be marked by significant territorial expansion, military campaigns against the Safavid Empire and the Mamluk Sultanate, and the establishment of Ottoman dominance in the Mediterranean and the Near East.
events.certainty_note: Selim I's ascension to the Ottoman throne in April 1512 is well-documented in Ottoman records.
cross_references: 1516-002 (Ottoman conquest of Egypt under Selim I)

events.title: Fifth Lateran Council convenes; Catholic Church reform efforts
events.description: The Fifth Lateran Council convened in Rome in 1512 under Pope Julius II to address issues of church reform and to counter the threat of schism within the Catholic Church. The council was called in response to the Council of Pisa (1511), which had been convened by French bishops in opposition to the pope. The Fifth Lateran Council lasted until 1517 and addressed various issues of ecclesiastical discipline, clerical corruption, and theological matters. However, the council's reform efforts were limited and did not address the fundamental issues that would lead to the Protestant Reformation. The council is significant as an example of the Catholic Church's attempts at internal reform before the Reformation, though these efforts proved insufficient to prevent the schism.
key_figures: Cardinals and bishops attending the council

sources.name: Contemporary accounts and later ecclesiastical histories

events.certainty_note: The Fifth Lateran Council's convening in 1512 is well-documented in papal records.

events.title: Battle of Ravenna; French victory in Italian Wars
events.description: French forces under King Francis I defeated a combined Spanish and papal army at the Battle of Ravenna on 11 April 1512. The battle was one of the largest and bloodiest of the Italian Wars, with estimates of 10,000–20,000 casualties. The French victory demonstrated the effectiveness of French artillery and military tactics. However, the French were unable to consolidate their victory due to lack of support from their allies and the intervention of other European powers. The battle is significant in the history of the Italian Wars, as it demonstrated the military capabilities of the French kingdom and the limitations of traditional military tactics against French artillery.
events.certainty_note: The Battle of Ravenna on 11 April 1512 is extensively documented in French, Spanish, and Italian sources.

events.title: Portuguese conquest of Goa; establishment of Portuguese India
events.description: Portuguese forces under Afonso de Albuquerque captured the city of Goa on the west coast of India in November 1510 (not 1512, but the consolidation and formal establishment of Portuguese rule occurred in 1512). Goa became the capital of the Portuguese Estado da Índia (State of India) and the centre of Portuguese colonial administration in Asia. The conquest involved defeating the local Bijapur Sultanate and establishing a fortified port. Goa remained under Portuguese control for over 450 years and became one of the most important European colonial possessions in Asia, serving as a hub for spice trade, missionary activity, and naval operations. The establishment of Goa marked a significant moment in the history of European colonialism in Asia.

sources.name: Portuguese royal records and Albuquerque's correspondence
Later Portuguese historiography (Gaspar Correia, Diogo do Couto)

events.certainty_note: The capture of Goa occurred in 1510, but its consolidation as the Portuguese capital in India was formalised in 1512.

events.title: Ming Dynasty's military campaigns against Mongol incursions
events.description: The Ming Dynasty, under the Zhengde Emperor, conducted military campaigns against Mongol and Jurchen incursions along China's northern frontier in 1512. These campaigns were part of ongoing efforts to secure the Great Wall and protect Ming territory from nomadic raids. The campaigns were generally successful in repelling incursions, though they did not result in permanent territorial gains. The Zhengde Emperor's military activities reflected the Ming Dynasty's continued military engagement with northern threats and the importance of frontier defence in Ming strategy.

sources.name: Ming Shilu (Veritable Records of the Ming)
Ming military records and garrison reports

events.certainty_note: Ming military campaigns in 1512 are documented in Ming records, but specific details and outcomes are sometimes vague.

events.title: Juan Ponce de León explores Puerto Rico and Florida
events.description: Spanish explorer Juan Ponce de León, commissioned by the Spanish Crown, explored Puerto Rico and the coast of Florida in 1512. Ponce de León was searching for gold and new territories to colonise. His expedition resulted in the establishment of Spanish settlements in Puerto Rico and the exploration of the Florida coast. Ponce de León's explorations contributed to Spanish knowledge of the Caribbean and North America and set the stage for future Spanish colonisation efforts in the region. The expeditions involved contact with indigenous peoples, often resulting in violence and the enslavement of indigenous populations.
key_figures: Indigenous Taíno and other Caribbean peoples

sources.name: Spanish royal records and later compilations
Peter Martyr d'Anghiera, De Orbe Novo

events.certainty_note: Ponce de León's exploration of Puerto Rico and Florida is documented in Spanish sources, but the exact dates and details of his expeditions vary between sources.

disconfirming_evidence: Selim I's ascension is sometimes presented as a sudden and dramatic change in Ottoman policy, but modern scholarship emphasises that Ottoman expansion was already underway under Bayezid II and that Selim I's reign represented a continuation and intensification of existing trends rather than a complete break. The Fifth Lateran Council is sometimes presented as a serious reform effort, but modern scholarship notes that it was limited in scope and did not address the fundamental issues that led to the Reformation. The Battle of Ravenna is sometimes presented as a decisive French victory that secured French dominance in Italy, but modern scholarship notes that the French were unable to consolidate their victory and that the battle's long-term significance was limited. Ponce de León's exploration of Florida is sometimes dated to 1513 or later; sources vary on the exact date.
historiographic_note: 1512 is well-documented in European sources, particularly for Spain, France, Portugal, and the Papacy. Ottoman sources are substantial but often written from the Ottoman perspective. Chinese sources are rich but primarily available in later compilations. Indigenous American perspectives are entirely absent from contemporary written records; European accounts are the only surviving documentation. The year reflects the global dominance of European documentation in surviving historical records.

graph_edges.note: Selim I's ascension led to Ottoman conquest of Egypt
Fifth Lateran Council's limited reforms contributed to conditions that led to Reformation
Battle of Ravenna contributed to French military reputation, leading to victory at Marignano
Portuguese expansion in Asia continued with consolidation of Goa
Ming Dynasty's military efforts on northern frontier were ongoing
Ponce de León's exploration of Florida led to later colonisation efforts
